mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and rising business investment, which boosts short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict returns during recovery phases or produces negative directional accuracy (<45%) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond the 3.0% annualized threshold, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >55% of threshold-crossing events or the signal shows no predictive edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or the signal predicts negative returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts combined with strong gross domestic product growth signal synchronized strength in residential construction and broad economic output, driving positive equity market momentum over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below its 6-month moving average indicates contracting manufacturing output and weakening business confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses directional accuracy below 40% in walk-forward testing.
